canoes.name: Chrysalis
canoes.description: Chrysalis is the canoe reborn. Pioneering a meshless fiber reinforced concrete design, the 2025-26 team paired a carbon negative mix and recycled sustainable materials with an AI-powered paddle trainer, taking 1st place overall at Regionals and 3rd at Nationals - the best finish in program history.

canoes.name: W-Train
canoes.description: The 2025 canoe, W-Train, carried the team through the 2024-25 season.

canoes.name: Rise
canoes.description: Rise stood for resilience and teamwork. The team achieved major aesthetic and structural improvements for the national showcase.

canoes.name: Jet
canoes.description: Jet emphasized lightweight innovation and aerodynamic flow, earning recognition for technical writing and design presentation.

canoes.name: Blossom
canoes.description: Blossom symbolized the team's return to form after the pandemic hiatus — a celebration of rebirth through collaborative spirit.

canoes.name: Golem
canoes.description: With Golem, the team focused on durability, strength, and balance, combining traditional casting methods with digital fabrication tools.

canoes.name: Voyager
canoes.description: Voyager was inspired by exploration — a design that pushed experimentation with advanced concrete composites and curing methods.

canoes.name: Bluefin
canoes.description: Bluefin emphasized hydrodynamic efficiency, with a streamlined hull and a renewed focus on paddling performance.

canoes.name: Spotlight
canoes.description: Spotlight brought creative innovation into the mix design and aesthetics, highlighting the team's growing technical versatility.

canoes.name: Sprout
canoes.description: Sprout represented the new generation of builders, integrating sustainability and eco-friendly design principles.

canoes.name: Koi
canoes.description: Koi featured colorful visual motifs and experimental aggregate mixtures inspired by fluid motion and aquatic balance.

canoes.name: Graffiti
canoes.description: Graffiti captured artistic expression, merging engineering with creative visuals for a bold and modern identity.

canoes.name: Maka
canoes.description: Maka emphasized balance between design and craft — its minimalist shape refined through precise formwork techniques.

canoes.name: Illa
canoes.description: Illa was a pivotal design, marking the team's transition to lighter, high-performance materials for faster competition speeds.

canoes.name: KingConCrete
canoes.description: KingConCrete became one of NYU's most iconic canoes, known for its strength and unique layered aggregate finish.

canoes.name: Fusion
canoes.description: Fusion was a competitive canoe of Poly's Concrete Canoe legacy — representing the team's early spirit.

canoes.name: Osiris
canoes.description: Osiris was the origin of NYU's modern Concrete Canoe legacy — representing the team's early dedication to precision and spirit.
